title: Google just had its first negative cash flow quarter due to massive AI spending
excerpt: Google continues to report big quarterly revenue, but its AI spending has skyrocketed.
content_html:
Google has reported its financial results for the second quarter of 2026 (PDF), and as usual, the search giant raked in an unfathomable amount of money. Google saw total revenue of $119.8 billion, beating analyst expectations by a comfortable margin. Despite that, the company’s stock has taken a hit. Along with all that revenue, Google has announced a further increase in its AI-fueled capital expenditures (or capex). The company is actually spending so much on AI infrastructure that it has negative cash flow for the first time.

Search was the largest chunk of Google’s income, accounting for $63.3 billion. Google Cloud pulled in $24.8 billion, a significant 23.8 percent increase from the first quarter. This shows there is massive demand for Google’s AI services. Google also earned $12.9 billion from its subscriptions, platforms, and devices portfolio, as well as $11.1 billion from YouTube ads. The company managed to goose that last one by more than 12 percent since last quarter as it made YouTube ads even longer.

A significant chunk of Google’s revenue comes from investments. When you subtract those non-cash earnings, Google’s operating cash flow for Q2 2026 was about $39.1 billion. That’s not the most the company has ever seen, but it’s a healthy 40 percent increase from Q2 2025. The problem is that Google’s spending has also gone up—a lot.

Before this latest round of financial updates, Google told investors it was expecting $180 billion to $190 billion in capital expenditures for 2026. Like other AI-obsessed tech behemoths, Google is burning cash on building and running the data centers powering its AI models. These numbers were already well above the $91 billion Google spent in 2025. The company now says it’s planning to spend as much as $205 billion on infrastructure in 2026.

As a result of its increasing AI demands, Google reports it spent $44.9 billion expanding its AI footprint in the second quarter, and you don’t need an accounting degree to know which number is larger. With $39.1 billion in cash income, this spending left Google with -$5.8 billion free cash flow.

sentences.en: Google has reported its financial results for the second quarter of 2026 (PDF), and as usual, the search giant raked in an unfathomable amount of money.
sentences.zh: 谷歌公布了 2026 年第二季度的财务业绩 (PDF)，和往常一样，这家搜索巨头赚得盆满钵满。

sentences.en: Google saw total revenue of $119.8 billion, beating analyst expectations by a comfortable margin.
sentences.zh: 谷歌总收入达 1,198 亿美元，大幅超出分析师预期。

sentences.en: Despite that, the company’s stock has taken a hit.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，该公司的股价还是受到了打击。

sentences.en: Along with all that revenue, Google has announced a further increase in its AI-fueled capital expenditures (or capex).
sentences.zh: 除了所有这些收入外，谷歌还宣布进一步增加人工智能驱动的资本支出（或资本支出）。

sentences.en: The company is actually spending so much on AI infrastructure that it has negative cash flow for the first time.
sentences.zh: 该公司实际上在人工智能基础设施上投入了大量资金，以至于首次出现负现金流。

The company managed to goose that last one by more than 12 percent since last quarter as it made YouTube ads even longer.

sentences.en: Search was the largest chunk of Google’s income, accounting for $63.3 billion.

sentences.en: Google Cloud pulled in $24.8 billion, a significant 23.8 percent increase from the first quarter.
sentences.zh: 谷歌云收入达 248 亿美元，较第一季度大幅增长 23.8%。

sentences.en: This shows there is massive demand for Google’s AI services.
sentences.zh: 这表明人们对谷歌的人工智能服务有巨大的需求。

sentences.en: Google also earned $12.9 billion from its subscriptions, platforms, and devices portfolio, as well as $11.1 billion from YouTube ads.
sentences.zh: 谷歌还从其订阅、平台和设备组合中赚取了 129 亿美元，从 YouTube 广告中赚取了 111 亿美元。

sentences.en: The company managed to goose that last one by more than 12 percent since last quarter as it made YouTube ads even longer.
sentences.zh: 自上季度以来，该公司成功将 Y​​ouTube 广告的持续时间延长了 12% 以上，因为该公司延长了 YouTube 广告的长度。

That’s not the most the company has ever seen, but it’s a healthy 40 percent increase from Q2 2025. The problem is that Google’s spending has also gone up—a lot.

sentences.en: A significant chunk of Google’s revenue comes from investments.
sentences.zh: 谷歌收入的很大一部分来自投资。

sentences.en: When you subtract those non-cash earnings, Google’s operating cash flow for Q2 2026 was about $39.1 billion.
sentences.zh: 扣除这些非现金收入后，谷歌 2026 年第二季度的运营现金流约为 391 亿美元。

sentences.en: That’s not the most the company has ever seen, but it’s a healthy 40 percent increase from Q2 2025.
sentences.zh: 这不是该公司有史以来的最高增幅，但与 2025 年第二季度相比，增长了 40%。

sentences.en: The problem is that Google’s spending has also gone up—a lot.
sentences.zh: 问题是谷歌的支出也增加了很多。

blocks.html_en: Before this latest round of financial updates, Google told investors it was expecting $180 billion to $190 billion in capital expenditures for 2026. Like other AI-obsessed tech behemoths, Google is burning cash on building and running the data centers powering its AI models. These numbers were already well above the $91 billion Google spent in 2025. The company now says it’s planning to spend as much as $205 billion on infrastructure in 2026.

sentences.en: Before this latest round of financial updates, Google told investors it was expecting $180 billion to $190 billion in capital expenditures for 2026.
sentences.zh: 在最新一轮财务更新之前，谷歌告诉投资者，预计 2026 年的资本支出将达到 1800 亿至 1900 亿美元。

sentences.en: Like other AI-obsessed tech behemoths, Google is burning cash on building and running the data centers powering its AI models.
sentences.zh: 与其他痴迷于人工智能的科技巨头一样，谷歌正在烧钱建设和运行为其人工智能模型提供支持的数据中心。

sentences.en: These numbers were already well above the $91 billion Google spent in 2025.
sentences.zh: 这些数字已经远远高于谷歌 2025 年花费的 910 亿美元。

sentences.en: The company now says it’s planning to spend as much as $205 billion on infrastructure in 2026.
sentences.zh: 该公司现在表示，计划到 2026 年在基础设施上花费高达 2050 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: As a result of its increasing AI demands, Google reports it spent $44.9 billion expanding its AI footprint in the second quarter, and you don’t need an accounting degree to know which number is larger. With $39.1 billion in cash income, this spending left Google with -$5.8 billion free cash flow.

sentences.en: As a result of its increasing AI demands, Google reports it spent $44.9 billion expanding its AI footprint in the second quarter, and you don’t need an accounting degree to know which number is larger.
sentences.zh: 由于人工智能需求不断增加，谷歌报告称，第二季度花费了 449 亿美元扩大人工智能足迹，你不需要会计学位就知道哪个数字更大。

sentences.en: With $39.1 billion in cash income, this spending left Google with -$5.8 billion free cash flow.
sentences.zh: 这笔支出为谷歌带来了 391 亿美元的现金收入，自由现金流为 -58 亿美元。

content_html_zh:
谷歌公布了 2026 年第二季度的财务业绩 (PDF)，和往常一样，这家搜索巨头赚得盆满钵满。 谷歌总收入达 1,198 亿美元，大幅超出分析师预期。 尽管如此，该公司的股价还是受到了打击。 除了所有这些收入外，谷歌还宣布进一步增加人工智能驱动的资本支出（或资本支出）。 该公司实际上在人工智能基础设施上投入了大量资金，以至于首次出现负现金流。

搜索是谷歌收入的最大部分，达 633 亿美元。 谷歌云收入达 248 亿美元，较第一季度大幅增长 23.8%。 这表明人们对谷歌的人工智能服务有巨大的需求。 谷歌还从其订阅、平台和设备组合中赚取了 129 亿美元，从 YouTube 广告中赚取了 111 亿美元。 自上季度以来，该公司成功将 Y​​ouTube 广告的持续时间延长了 12% 以上，因为该公司延长了 YouTube 广告的长度。

谷歌收入的很大一部分来自投资。 扣除这些非现金收入后，谷歌 2026 年第二季度的运营现金流约为 391 亿美元。 这不是该公司有史以来的最高增幅，但与 2025 年第二季度相比，增长了 40%。 问题是谷歌的支出也增加了很多。

在最新一轮财务更新之前，谷歌告诉投资者，预计 2026 年的资本支出将达到 1800 亿至 1900 亿美元。 与其他痴迷于人工智能的科技巨头一样，谷歌正在烧钱建设和运行为其人工智能模型提供支持的数据中心。 这些数字已经远远高于谷歌 2025 年花费的 910 亿美元。 该公司现在表示，计划到 2026 年在基础设施上花费高达 2050 亿美元。

由于人工智能需求不断增加，谷歌报告称，第二季度花费了 449 亿美元扩大人工智能足迹，你不需要会计学位就知道哪个数字更大。 这笔支出为谷歌带来了 391 亿美元的现金收入，自由现金流为 -58 亿美元。
